items.title: Live off the Land? How About Bringing Your Own Island? An Overview of UNC1945
items.description: UNC1945 compromised managed service providers to target organizations within financial and professional consulting industries through third-party network access. The actor demonstrated advanced capabilities across Oracle Solaris, Windows, and Linux systems, utilizing custom virtual machines pre-loaded with post-exploitation tools. Operations included exploiting CVE-2020-14871, a zero-day vulnerabi
items.ai_summary: UNC1945, a sophisticated threat actor, compromised managed service providers to target organizations in the financial and professional consulting industries through third-party network access, leveraging advanced capabilities across Oracle Solaris, Windows, and Linux systems. The actor utilized custom virtual machines pre-loaded with post-exploitation tools, exploiting vulnerabilities such as CVE-2020-14871, a zero-day vulnerability in Oracle WebLogic Server. If exploited, this vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary code, potentially leading to a full system compromise. To remediate, defenders should apply the Oracle WebLogic Server patch for CVE-2020-14871, ensuring they update to a version later than 10.3.6.0.0 or 12.2.1.3.0, and restrict access to the administrative console. Additionally, implementing a robust network segmentation strategy and monitoring for suspicious activity can help mitigate the impact of such attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1199| B[Trusted Relationship]:::tactic
    B -->|T1078| C[Valid Accounts]:::tactic
    C -->|T1210| D[Exploitation of Remote Services]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Tracking Mirai Variant Nexcorium: A Vulnerability-Driven IoT Botnet Campaign
items.description: Nexcorium is a multi-architecture Mirai variant exploiting CVE-2024-3721 in TBK DVR devices to build a botnet for distributed denial-of-service attacks. The campaign, attributed to Nexus Team based on custom HTTP headers, uses OS command injection to deliver malware across ARM, MIPS, and x86-64 architectures. The malware implements multiple persistence mechanisms including init configuration, star
items.ai_summary: The Nexcorium campaign is a Mirai variant that exploits a vulnerability in TBK DVR devices, specifically targeting CVE-2024-3721, to build a botnet for distributed denial-of-service attacks. This campaign is attributed to the Nexus Team based on custom HTTP headers and utilizes OS command injection to deliver malware across various architectures, including ARM, MIPS, and x86-64. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to significant disruptions in service, affecting organizations that rely on these devices. To remediate, defenders should immediately update their TBK DVR devices to the latest firmware version and ensure that all devices are configured to use secure protocols for communication. Additionally, implementing a network segmentation strategy and regularly monitoring for suspicious traffic can help mitigate the impact of this threat.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1583.005| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1490| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: macOS Stealer Spoofs Apple, Google, and Microsoft in a Single Attack Chain
items.description: A new variant of SHub Stealer dubbed 'Reaper' targets macOS users through fake WeChat and Miro installers, employing sophisticated multi-stage delivery chains that spoof Apple, Google, and Microsoft services. The malware leverages the applescript:// URL scheme to bypass Terminal-based defenses, conducting extensive fingerprinting and anti-analysis checks before execution. Reaper harvests browser c
items.ai_summary: The Reaper variant of the SHub Stealer malware targets macOS users through fake WeChat and Miro installers, utilizing the applescript:// URL scheme to bypass Terminal-based defenses. This sophisticated multi-stage attack chain spoofs Apple, Google, and Microsoft services, conducting extensive fingerprinting and anti-analysis checks before execution. If exploited, Reaper can harvest browser credentials, steal or forge authentication certificates, and gather victim host information, affecting individuals and organizations using macOS systems. To remediate, defenders should update their macOS systems to the latest version, ensure that all software is obtained from trusted sources, and implement robust security controls such as multi-factor authentication and regular security audits. Additionally, users should be cautious when clicking on links or installing software from untrusted sources, and consider implementing a web application firewall to detect and prevent similar attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1104| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.002| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1592| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1649| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: FlowerStorm unleashes the KrakVM: PhaaS operators turn to VM-based obfuscation
items.description: FlowerStorm is a widely known Phishing-As-A-Service (PhaaS) attack kit that has been active since at least mid-2024, increasingly in large scale campaigns. FlowerStorm performs targeted, complex collection of a victim’s credentials, including the management of multi-factor authentication (MFA).
items.ai_summary: The FlowerStorm PhaaS attack kit has been leveraging the KrakVM to obfuscate its phishing campaigns, making it challenging for defenders to detect and mitigate. This kit has been active since mid-2024 and is known for its complex collection of victim credentials, including bypassing multi-factor authentication (MFA). The attack vector typically involves targeted phishing emails or messages that trick victims into divulging sensitive information. To remediate, defenders should implement robust email filtering solutions, such as those that can detect and block phishing attempts based on behavioral analysis, and ensure all employees undergo regular security awareness training to recognize phishing attempts. Additionally, enabling MFA with a time-based one-time password (TOTP) and ensuring that all systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches can help mitigate the impact of such attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1027| C[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1204| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Vidar v1.5 in Go: same family, new language, heavy sandbox checks
items.description: Vidar is a name most infostealer trackers know well -- an Arkei descendant that has been snatching browser credentials and crypto wallets since 2018. It usually ships as a .NET binary or a C++ PE. The v1.5 sample we pulled from Triage on May 13, 2026 is neither. It is a 7 MB Go 1.25.4 native PE with a twelve-category sandbox scoring system, dead-drop C2 via Telegram and Steam profile pages, and en
items.ai_summary: The Vidar v1.5 infostealer, written in Go, has been identified as a significant threat due to its advanced sandbox evasion techniques and ability to steal sensitive information such as browser credentials and crypto wallets. This malware, a descendant of the Arkei family, has been active since 2018 and typically spreads through .NET or C++ binaries, but the latest sample is a 7 MB Go 1.25.4 native PE. To mitigate this threat, defenders should implement robust application layer protocol monitoring, as Vidar utilizes T1071 to communicate with its command and control servers, and ensure that all web browsers and crypto wallet software are updated to the latest versions, with particular attention to securing authentication certificates to prevent T1649 exploits. Additionally, monitoring for suspicious activity related to T1555.003, such as unauthorized access to web browser credentials, is crucial. Specific remediation steps include configuring web browsers to use secure protocols and enabling two-factor authentication for all sensitive accounts.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1071| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1555.003| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1649| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1071| E[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Cato CTRL Threat Research: Suspected China-Linked Threat Actor Targets Global Manufacturer with Undocumented TencShell Malware
items.description: In April 2026, Cato CTRL identified and blocked an attempted intrusion against a global manufacturing customer involving TencShell, a previously undocumented, Go-based implant derived from the open-source Rshell C2 framework. The activity appeared in traffic associated with a third-party user connected to the customer environment.
items.ai_summary: The suspected China-linked threat actor utilized the undocumented TencShell malware, a Go-based implant derived from the open-source Rshell C2 framework, to target a global manufacturer. The attack vector involved exploiting public-facing applications, as indicated by the T1190 TTP, and leveraging application layer protocols, as seen in T1071. If successfully exploited, this could lead to unauthorized access and potential data breaches, affecting the confidentiality and integrity of the manufacturer's data. To remediate, defenders should implement robust network traffic monitoring, focusing on unusual application layer protocol activity, and ensure all public-facing applications are securely configured, with particular emphasis on those using Go-based frameworks. Additionally, defenders should consider implementing a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent similar exploits, and ensure all systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches, although no specific CVE is associated with this threat.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1071| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Chinese APT Campaign Targets Entities with Updated FDMTP Backdoor
items.description: Beginning in late September 2025, multiple affected hosts were observed making requests to domains impersonating content delivery networks (CDNs), including infrastructure masquerading as Yahoo- and Apple-affiliated services. Across these cases, Darktrace identified a consistent behavioral execution pattern: the retrieval of legitimate binaries alongside malicious Dynamic Link Libraries (DLLs), en
items.ai_summary: The Chinese APT campaign utilizes an updated FDMTP backdoor to target entities, with the threat actor employing masquerading techniques to impersonate content delivery networks, including those affiliated with Yahoo and Apple. The attack vector involves the retrieval of legitimate binaries alongside malicious Dynamic Link Libraries (DLLs), enabling the threat actor to establish remote access. If exploited, the backdoor allows for unauthorized access and potential data exfiltration, with multiple hosts observed making requests to the impersonated domains. To remediate, defenders should implement robust network traffic monitoring and inspect for suspicious DLLs, updating their security software to the latest versions, such as Darktrace's Enterprise Immune System, and configuring their systems to block unknown or untrusted binaries. Additionally, defenders should verify the authenticity of CDN services and restrict access to only trusted sources.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1036| B[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    B -->|T1219| C[Command and Control]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Discovery]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Spring harvest - Leek Likho group's campaign to hunt for documents
items.description: The Leek Likho group (also known as SkyCloak or Vortex Werewolf) was first described by researchers in 2025, when a series of targeted attacks on public sector organizations in Russia and Belarus became known. This campaign was called Operation SkyCloak. We observed the continuation of its activity during February-April 2026, and also discovered a new technique that attackers use to filter files.
items.ai_summary: The Leek Likho group, also known as SkyCloak or Vortex Werewolf, has been observed conducting targeted attacks on public sector organizations in Russia and Belarus, utilizing techniques such as email account compromise (T1586.002) and authentication certificate theft or forgery (T1649) to gain initial access. The attackers then use these compromised accounts to steal sensitive documents, and in some cases, encrypt data for impact (T1486), likely for extortion or disruption purposes. To defend against this campaign, organizations should implement multi-factor authentication for all email accounts, regularly review and rotate authentication certificates, and ensure that all employees are trained to identify and report suspicious email activity. Additionally, organizations should consider implementing a data loss prevention (DLP) solution to detect and prevent sensitive document exfiltration. By taking these specific remediation steps, defenders can reduce the risk of falling victim to this campaign.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1586.002| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1649| C[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    C -->|T1486| D[Impact]:::tactic
    D -->|T107| E[Collection]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Copycat hits another npm package
items.description: A Shai-Hulud copycat worm has infected the npm package chalk-tempalte, appearing just five days after the original worm was open-sourced by its creators. The same threat actor also published three additional malicious npm packages containing infostealer code: @deadcode09284814/axios-util, axois-utils, and color-style-utils. These packages collectively received 2,678 weekly downloads and contain va
items.ai_summary: The Shai-Hulud copycat worm has infected the npm package chalk-tempalte, leveraging the same tactics as the original worm, which was open-sourced by its creators just five days prior. This threat actor has also published three additional malicious npm packages, @deadcode09284814/axios-util, axois-utils, and color-style-utils, containing infostealer code, with a combined total of 2,678 weekly downloads. The attack vector involves exploiting public-facing applications and compromising software dependencies, specifically through T1190 and T1195.001 tactics. To remediate, defenders should immediately remove the affected packages and update their npm configurations to prevent further exploitation, ensuring that all dependencies are verified and up-to-date, and monitoring for suspicious activity. Additionally, developers should be cautious when installing new packages and verify the authenticity of the packages before use.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1195.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Active Supply Chain Attack Compromises Packages on npm
items.description: An active npm supply chain attack has compromised packages in the @antv ecosystem, affecting the maintainer account 'atool'. The attack is part of the Mini Shai-Hulud campaign, involving 639 compromised package versions across 323 unique packages. Notable affected packages include echarts-for-react with 1.1 million weekly downloads, and widely-used @antv packages for data visualization. The malwar
items.ai_summary: The active supply chain attack on npm has compromised 639 package versions across 323 unique packages in the @antv ecosystem, including echarts-for-react with 1.1 million weekly downloads. The attack, part of the Mini Shai-Hulud campaign, involves the maintainer account 'atool' and utilizes the T1195 supply chain compromise tactic. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to malicious code execution, potentially affecting numerous downstream applications that rely on these packages. To remediate, defenders should immediately remove and replace affected packages with known good versions, and monitor their applications for suspicious activity. Additionally, developers should verify the integrity of their dependencies and consider using tools like npm audit or snyk to identify and address potential vulnerabilities.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2021-47922: Slider by Soliloquy 2.6.2 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability t...
items.description: Slider by Soliloquy 2.6.2 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows authenticated attackers to inject malicious scripts through the title parameter. Attackers can add JavaScript payloads in the title field when creating or editing sliders, which executes in the browsers of users viewing the slider on both administrative and frontend pages.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2021-47922 is a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in Slider by Soliloquy version 2.6.2, allowing authenticated attackers to inject malicious JavaScript payloads through the title parameter when creating or editing sliders. This vulnerability can be exploited by adding JavaScript code in the title field, which will execute in the browsers of users viewing the slider on both administrative and frontend pages. The real-world impact of this vulnerability includes the potential for attackers to steal user sessions, perform unauthorized actions, or inject malware. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to Slider by Soliloquy version 2.6.3 or later, and ensure that all instances of the plugin are updated to the latest version. Additionally, users can mitigate the vulnerability by restricting access to the slider creation and editing functionality to trusted users only.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.007| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2021-47910: AccessPress Social Icons 1.8.2 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerabil...
items.description: AccessPress Social Icons 1.8.2 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows authenticated attackers to inject malicious scripts by entering JavaScript payloads into the 'icon title' field. Attackers can store XSS payloads like image tags with onerror event handlers that execute when the plugin page is viewed, affecting all users who access the plugin interface.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2021-47910 is a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in AccessPress Social Icons version 1.8.2, allowing authenticated attackers to inject malicious scripts by entering JavaScript payloads into the 'icon title' field. This can lead to the execution of arbitrary code when the plugin page is viewed, affecting all users who access the interface. The vulnerability can be exploited using techniques such as T1059.007, where attackers use JavaScript to inject malicious code, and T1546, which involves event-triggered execution. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to a version of AccessPress Social Icons that is later than 1.8.2, and ensure that all input fields are properly sanitized to prevent code injection. Additionally, defenders can implement security controls such as output encoding and input validation to prevent similar attacks.
items.workflow_graph:

    B -->|T1546| C[Execution]:::tactic

items.title: CVE-2021-47907: Rocket LMS 1.1 contains a persistent cross-site scripting vulnerability in the s...
items.description: Rocket LMS 1.1 contains a persistent cross-site scripting vulnerability in the support ticket module that allows authenticated users to inject malicious script code through the title parameter. Attackers can submit support tickets with embedded HTML/JavaScript payloads that execute in the browsers of other users viewing the message history, enabling session hijacking and phishing attacks.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2021-47907 is a persistent cross-site scripting vulnerability in Rocket LMS version 1.1, specifically within the support ticket module, allowing authenticated users to inject malicious script code through the title parameter. This vulnerability enables attackers to submit support tickets with embedded HTML/JavaScript payloads that execute in the browsers of other users viewing the message history, leading to session hijacking and phishing attacks. The real-world impact includes the potential for attackers to steal web session cookies and perform remote service session hijacking, as indicated by the detected TTPs T1566, T1059.007, T1539, and T1563. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update Rocket LMS to a version later than 1.1 and ensure that all support ticket module inputs are properly sanitized to prevent code injection. Additionally, implementing a Web Application Firewall (WAF) with rules to detect and prevent JavaScript injection can provide an extra layer of protection.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR

    B -->|T1059.007| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1539| D[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    D -->|T1563| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.description: A vulnerability was identified in Industrial Application Software IAS Canias ERP 8.03. This impacts an unknown function of the component Login RMI Interface. The manipulation of the argument clientVersion leads to improper authentication. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-8244 is a vulnerability in Industrial Application Software IAS Canias ERP version 8.03, specifically affecting the Login RMI Interface component. The vulnerability allows for improper authentication due to the manipulation of the clientVersion argument, which can be exploited remotely. If successfully exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data and systems, potentially affecting industrial control systems and manufacturing operations. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update to IAS Canias ERP version 8.04 or later, and ensure that all remote access to the Login RMI Interface is properly authenticated and authorized. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious activity and implement network segmentation to limit the potential impact of a successful exploit.

Executing a manipulation can lead to use of hard-coded cryptographic key . The attack may be performed from remote. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-8243 is a vulnerability in Industrial Application Software IAS Canias ERP version 8.03, specifically affecting the JNLP Deployment Endpoint component. The vulnerability allows for the use of a hard-coded cryptographic key, which can be exploited remotely. If successfully exploited, this could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data, potentially affecting industrial control systems and manufacturing operations. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update to a patched version of IAS Canias ERP, if available, and consider implementing additional security controls such as encryption and secure authentication mechanisms. In the absence of a vendor-provided patch, defenders may need to implement temporary workarounds, such as restricting access to the JNLP Deployment Endpoint or monitoring for suspicious activity.

It said the scope of the incident is limited to the Grafana Labs GitHub environment, which includes public and private source code along with internal GitHub repositories. items.title: Verizon DBIR 2026: Vulnerability Exploitation Overtakes Credential Theft as Top Breach Vector
items.description: Verizon’s 2026 DBIR finds vulnerability exploitation has overtaken credential abuse as the leading breach vector, as AI accelerates attacks, patching delays worsen, and ransomware and third-party compromises continue to surge. The post Verizon DBIR 2026: Vulnerability Exploitation Overtakes Credential Theft as Top Breach Vector appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Max-severity flaw in ChromaDB for AI apps allows server hijacking
items.description: A max-severity vulnerability in the latest Python FastAPI version of the ChromaDB project allows unauthenticated attackers to run arbitrary code on exposed servers. [...]

items.title: What Will Make AI BOMs Real?
items.description: A brief overview of the forces at play that will get more organizations on board with creating and consuming AI bill of materials (BOMs).

items.title: Verizon DBIR: Enterprises Face a Dangerous Vulnerability Glut
items.description: Verizon's "2026 Data Breach Investigations Report" ("DBIR") finds that exploits are now involved in 31% of initial access for breaches, while patching lags too far behind the bad guys.

items.title: Cybercrime service disrupted for abusing Microsoft platform to sign malware
items.description: Microsoft says it has disrupted a malware-signing-as-a-service (MSaaS) operation that abused the company's Artifact Signing service to generate fraudulent code-signing certificates used by ransomware gangs and other cybercriminals. [...]

items.title: Windows Zero-Day Barrage Continues After Patch Tuesday
items.description: YellowKey, GreenPlasma, and MiniPlasma add to the growing list of vulnerabilities a security researcher disclosed over the past six weeks.

items.title: Discord rolls out end-to-end encryption on voice, video calls
items.description: Discord announced that all voice and video calls through the communication platform are now protected by default with end-to-end encryption (E2EE). [...]

items.title: CISA Exposes Secrets, Credentials in 'Private' Repo
items.description: The agency's GitHub repository, publicly available since November 2025, was ironically named "Private-CISA."

items.title: Stealer Spoofs Google, Microsoft & Apple, Then Backdoors macOS
items.description: The SHub Reaper stealer, which hides behind fake WeChat and Miro installers, marks a shift from ClickFix social engineering to Apple script-based execution.

items.title: FBI: Americans lost over $388 million to scams using crypto ATMs in 2025
items.description: The FBI says Americans have lost over $388 million last year to scams using cryptocurrency kiosks, also known as crypto ATMs or Bitcoin ATMs. [...]

items.title: Microsoft Self-Service Password Reset abused in Azure data theft attacks
items.description: A threat actor targeting Microsoft 365 and Azure production environments is stealing data in attacks that abuse legitimate applications and administration features. [...]

items.title: Huawei zero-day attack behind last year’s crash of Luxembourg's entire telecoms network
items.description: There is no evidence that the incident has recurred, but the flaw remains unexplained and has not been publicly acknowledged by the company.

items.title: UK regulator to require tech firms to tackle deepfakes, non-consensual intimate images
items.description: The regulator’s announcement said the change is being made due to the “urgent need to better protect women and girls online.”

items.title: Trapdoor Android Ad Fraud Scheme Hit 659 Million Daily Bid Requests Using 455 Apps
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new ad fraud and malvertising operation dubbed Trapdoor targeting Android device users. The activity, per HUMAN's Satori Threat Intelligence and Research Team, encompassed 455 malicious Android apps and 183 threat actor-owned command-and-control (C2) domains, turning the infrastructure into a pipeline for multi-stage fraud. "Users

items.title: Microsoft disrupts Fox Tempest malware-signing-as-a-service platform tied to ransomware gangs
items.description: The company unsealed a legal case in U.S. District Court on Tuesday detailing the disruption of Fox Tempest — a popular service that has operated since May 2025 and provides cybercriminals with code signing tools.

items.title: Microsoft plans to improve Windows 11 driver quality in 2026
items.description: Microsoft plans to raise the quality bar of Windows 11 drivers, as drivers "sit at the heart of every Windows experience" and connect the OS to the "silicon, components, and peripherals." [...]

items.title: Drupal to Patch Highly Critical Vulnerability at Risk of Quick Exploitation
items.description: Drupal says attackers may develop an exploit for the vulnerability within hours or days. The post Drupal to Patch Highly Critical Vulnerability at Risk of Quick Exploitation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft blames macOS update for undismissible Teams location prompts
items.description: Microsoft has confirmed user reports that the Teams team collaboration app is displaying non-dismissible location prompts on some macOS systems. [...]

items.title: Microsoft Disrupts Malware-Signing Service Run by ‘Fox Tempest’
items.description: Fox Tempest provides a service that cybercriminals use to distribute ransomware and other malware disguised as legitimate software. The post Microsoft Disrupts Malware-Signing Service Run by ‘Fox Tempest’ appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: DirtyDecrypt PoC Released for Linux Kernel CVE-2026-31635 LPE Vulnerability
items.description: Proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit code has now been released for a recently patched security flaw in the Linux kernel that could allow for local privilege escalation (LPE). Dubbed DirtyDecrypt (aka DirtyCBC), the vulnerability was discovered and reported by the Zellic and V12 security team on May 9, 2026, only to be informed by the maintainers that it was a duplicate of a vulnerability that had

items.title: New Shai-Hulud malware wave compromises 600 npm packages
items.description: Threat actors earlier today published more than 600 malicious packages to the Node Package Manager (npm) index as part of a new Shai-Hulud supply-chain campaign. [...]

items.title: 7-Eleven confirms data breach claimed by the ShinyHunters gang
items.description: Convenience store chain giant 7-Eleven confirmed that its systems were breached in a cyberattack claimed by the ShinyHunters extortion group last month. [...]

items.title: Looking Back, Looking Forward: Digesting a Dynamic Bouillabaisse of Cyber Evolution
items.description: Dark Reading editors reflect on two decades of dramatic change — from perimeter defense to assume-breach strategies — and warn that while AI, cloud, and COVID-19 have transformed the threat landscape, organizations are still failing at fundamental security hygiene that could stop sophisticated attacks in their tracks.

items.title: Legacy Windows Tool MSHTA Fuels Surge in Silent Malware Attacks
items.description: Attackers are increasingly abusing Microsoft’s decades-old MSHTA utility to stealthily deliver stealers, loaders, and persistent malware through phishing, fake software downloads, and LOLBIN-based attack chains. The post Legacy Windows Tool MSHTA Fuels Surge in Silent Malware Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Unpatched ChromaDB Vulnerability Can Lead to Server Takeover
items.description: The security defect can be exploited remotely, without authentication, to execute arbitrary code and leak sensitive information. The post Unpatched ChromaDB Vulnerability Can Lead to Server Takeover appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: B1ack’s Stash Marketplace Gives Away 4.6 Million Stolen Credit Cards
items.description: The stolen credit card data was released as a free download, allegedly in response to seller misconduct. The post B1ack’s Stash Marketplace Gives Away 4.6 Million Stolen Credit Cards appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: The New Phishing Click: How OAuth Consent Bypasses MFA
items.description: In February 2026, a phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform called EvilTokens went live. Within five weeks, it had compromised more than 340 Microsoft 365 organizations across five countries.  The targets of the platform received a message asking them to enter a short code at microsoft.com/devicelogin and complete their normal MFA challenge, then walked away believing they had verified a

items.title: Cyber Resilience is the New Business Continuity Plan
items.description: The organizations best prepared to face disruption are those that align security, continuity and risk management around what the business cannot afford to lose. The post Cyber Resilience is the New Business Continuity Plan appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Drupal to Release Urgent Core Security Updates on May 20, Sites Told to Prepare
items.description: Drupal has issued an alert stating that it intends to release a "core security release" for all supported branches on May 20, 2026, from 5-9 p.m. UTC. "The Drupal Security Team urges you to reserve time for core updates at that time because exploits might be developed within hours or days," the maintainers of the PHP-based content management system (CMS) said. "Not all configurations are

items.title: 201 Arrested in Crackdown on Cybercrime in Middle East, North Africa
items.description: The 13-country effort, named Operation Ramz, targeted cyber threats in the Middle East and North Africa region. The post 201 Arrested in Crackdown on Cybercrime in Middle East, North Africa appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: PoC Released for DirtyDecrypt Linux Kernel Vulnerability
items.description: Patched in April, the underlying vulnerability allows local attackers to elevate their privileges to root. The post PoC Released for DirtyDecrypt Linux Kernel Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: SEPPMail Secure E-Mail Gateway Vulnerabilities Enable RCE and Mail Traffic Access
items.description: Critical security vulnerabilities have been disclosed in SEPPMail Secure E-Mail Gateway, an enterprise-grade email security solution, that could be exploited to achieve remote code execution and enable an attacker to read arbitrary mails from the virtual appliance. "These vulnerabilities could have been exploited to read all mail traffic or as an entry vector into the internal network,"

items.title: Compromised Nx Console 18.95.0 Targeted VS Code Developers with Credential Stealer
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a compromised version of the Nx Console extension that was published to the Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) Marketplace. The extension in question is rwl.angular-console (version 18.95.0), a popular user interface and plugin for code editors like VS Code, Cursor, and JetBrains. The VS Code extension has more than 2.2 million installations. The Open

items.title: Critical Vulnerability Exposes Industrial Robot Fleets to Hacking
items.description: The vulnerability, CVE-2026-8153, affects Universal Robots PolyScope 5 and it can be exploited for OS command injection. The post Critical Vulnerability Exposes Industrial Robot Fleets to Hacking appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Popular GitHub Action Tags Redirected to Imposter Commit to Steal CI/CD Credentials
items.description: In yet another software supply chain attack, threat actors have compromised the popular GitHub Actions workflow, actions-cool/issues-helper, to run malicious code that harvests sensitive credentials and exfiltrates them to an attacker-controlled server. "Every existing tag in the repository has been moved to point to an imposter commit that does not appear in the action's normal commit history,

items.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Pushes Malicious AntV npm Packages via Compromised Maintainer Account
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a fresh software supply chain attack campaign that has compromised various npm packages associated with the @antv ecosystem as part of the ongoing Mini Shai-Hulud attack wave. "The attack affects packages tied to the npm maintainer account atool, including echarts-for-react, a widely used React wrapper for Apache ECharts with roughly 1.1 million weekly

items.title: Is 2026 the Year AI Bills of Materials Get Real?
items.description: Understanding AI BOMs and where they fit into risk management for artificial intelligence.

items.title: Microsoft Exchange Zero-Day Under Attack, No Patch Available
items.description: CVE-2026-42897 stems from a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability and can allow an attacker to compromise Outlook Web Access (OWA) mailboxes.

items.title: 'Claw Chain' Vulnerabilities Threaten OpenClaw Deployments
items.description: The now-patched vulnerabilities in the rapidly growing AI agent framework allow attackers to steal credentials, escalate privileges, and maintain persistence.

items.title: More than 200 arrested in cyber raids aimed at Middle East scam networks
items.description: Investigators found hundreds of compromised devices that were used as part of the cybercriminal operation and notified device owners as part of the raids.

items.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Activity Through 2026-05-17, (Mon, May 18th)
items.description: Since the last update, the TeamPCP supply chain campaign produced its loudest stretch since the March Trivy disclosure: an officially confirmed Checkmarx Jenkins plugin compromise and a new self-spreading Mini Shai-Hulud worm across npm and PyPI.

items.title: Shai-Hulud Worm Clones Spread After Code Release
items.description: The release of Shai-Hulud source code spells trouble for software developers as researchers worry the self-replicating worm could scale.

items.title: Grafana refuses to pay ransom after codebase theft
items.description: On Saturday night, the company released a statement confirming the incident and outlining their decision not to pay a ransom issued by the hackers behind the attack.

items.title: [Guest Diary]  New Malware Libraries means New Signatures, (Fri, May 15th)

items.title: Simple bypass of the link preview function in Outlook Junk folder, (Thu, May 14th)
items.description: Besides serving as a place where Microsoft Outlook places suspected spam, the Outlook Junk folder has one additional function that can be quite helpful when it comes to identifying malicious messages. Any e-mail placed in this folder is stripped of all formatting, and destinations of all links included in the message become visible to the user, as you can see in the following images which show the same e-mail when it is placed in the inbox, and when it is placed in the Junk folder.

items.description: .. if â€œunproxyableâ€ is a word that is ..

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
